title: Skill Discovery, Trigger Descriptions, and Boundaries · Transfer Build
summary: Make explicit and implicit invocation reliable without false positives. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a trigger evaluation dataset with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Front-load use cases and trigger terms in a concise skill description.
Write diverse prompts that should activate the skill.
Write adjacent prompts that must not activate the skill.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-trigger-evals-1pm23c: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Trigger Evals into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-trigger-evals-1pm23c:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Trigger Evals failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A skill that works after manual selection needs no trigger tests.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure missed triggers, false triggers, and competing-skill selection.

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Trigger Evals condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Trigger Evals applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure missed triggers, false triggers, and competing-skill selection.
